mechanism_narrative: Persistently elevated unemployment (above 8%) indicates ongoing labor market slack, weakening consumer spending and earnings growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is an always-on signal given current unemployment at 9.5%.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.5% well above natural rate estimates
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is above 8% over the next two years, or the rank IC of UNRATE level against 20-day returns is not significantly negative (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rising above 600,000 units signals a meaningful recovery in residential investment, which boosts construction employment and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. The trigger is a monthly cross above this threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST=549 is below threshold but recovering from trough
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when HOUST crosses above 600, or average returns are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicate broad-based demand growth that lifts corporate earnings and equity valuations. This interaction-driven signal strengthens the confidence in the expansion.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 0 and RSAFS month-over-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:Both series have shown positive trends since early 2010
failure_condition: Median 20-day forward return following joint positive readings is not significantly above zero, or the proportion of positive returns is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In the zero lower bound regime (FEDFUNDS < 1%), rising inflation (CPIAUCSL increase) signals improving demand without immediate tightening risk, boosting equities over a 60-day horizon. This regime-conditional effect reverses once rates normalize.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.18% is near zero, CPIAUCSL=217.6 shows moderate inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative more than half the time when inflation rises while at the ZLB, or the average return is not significantly positive (one-sided test p<0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production indicate expanding manufacturing activity and supply capacity, which bolsters corporate profits and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon. This is an always-on trend signal when INDPRO is rising.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has been rising from trough and is at 92.5
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO 3-month change vs 60-day forward returns is ≤ 0 over any 2-year rolling window, or the average forward return in rising INDPRO periods is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll gains exceeding 150,000 in a month signal robust job creation that boosts consumer sentiment and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. The threshold is crossed when monthly net additions surpass this level.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has been volatile but trending positive in 2010
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following a >150K payroll print are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p<0.10) or have a win rate below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint expansion in industrial production and retail sales indicates synchronized growth across manufacturing and consumption, a powerful signal for improving corporate earnings. This interaction-driven signal implies broad-based demand and supply strength.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0 and RSAFS month-over-month change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:Both have shown positive momentum in recent months
failure_condition: Median 20-day forward return when both rise is not greater than the unconditional median, or joint positive signal has a lower Sharpe ratio than the single-series signals.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime (UNRATE > 8%), a rapid monthly decline in unemployment rate greater than 0.2 percentage points signals a decisive labor market turnaround that boosts equity prices over the next 5 trading days. The effect weakens at lower unemployment levels.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=9.5% is high, and large declines are rare but significant
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following rapid UNRATE drops in high-unemployment regime are not positive more than 60% of the time, or the signal has negative average returns over a 2-year period.
